description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane with a bright orange and black color scheme, featuring two stacked wings connected by vertical struts. The fuselage is cylindrical and tapers towards the tail, with a large, exposed wooden propeller mounted at the front. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, likely designed for a single pilot. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, characteristic of early 20th-century aircraft. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a conventional tail design. The surface shows signs of wear and weathering, giving it a rugged, utilitarian appearance.
description: A vintage orange and black biplane with an open cockpit, exposed propeller, and classic taildragger landing gear.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the dual stacked wings, central fuselage, and the full span of the tail and wings.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the large wooden propeller, open cockpit, and the symmetrical biplane wing structure.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view displaying the underside of the wings, landing gear, and the tail skid.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail assembly, including the single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with an exposed wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing.
